bugs_found.description: signNoZero<float> returns 0 for NaN, violates ne_zero.
negate<int16_t> is not a global involution. negate(negate(-32767))=-32768. INT16_MAX special case wrong.

notes: set tactic NOT available in Lean 4 stdlib
norm_num tactic NOT available in Lean 4 stdlib
linarith NOT available, ring NOT available
split_ifs NOT available in Lean 4 stdlib - use by_cases
le_rfl NOT available - use Int.le_refl _ or Nat.le_refl _
variable {w hw} at module level conflicts with explicit (w : Nat) (hw : 0 < w) in function signatures
List.get_mem has signature: get l i in l (not get_mem l i _)
List.mem_replicate: a in replicate n x iff n != 0 and a = x; extract with .2
omega works for Nat.mod_lt and modular arithmetic
Unused theorem parameters: prefix with _ (e.g. _h, _ha0) to suppress linter warning
Unused simp args: remove from simp only [...] list to avoid linter warnings
List.mergeSort uses Bool comparator: def intLE (a b : Int) : Bool := if a <= b then true else false
intLE_iff: intLE a b = true <-> a <= b (needed for pairwise -> Prop conversion)
sorted_getElem_le: uses pairwise_mergeSort + pairwise_iff_getElem for i <= j ordering
List.pairwise_mergeSort: takes (trans) (total) (l) - returns Pairwise on mergeSort result
List.getElem_replicate: (replicate n a)[i] = a for i < n
For constant list median proof: use List.mem_mergeSort + List.mem_replicate + .2
mfInsert_head_wraps: omega cannot prove (window-1+1) % window = 0; use Nat.sub_add_cancel + rw [Nat.mod_self]
native_decide works for List.mergeSort with Int and concrete lists (window=3 or window=5)
decide does NOT reduce length of mergeSort result - use native_decide for index bounds in examples
